title: "multiple <key>=<value> ... variables in ENV instruction" generate one variables in the generated image
body:
### Issue Description

4 environment variable are expected for below according to [Env](https://docs.docker.com/reference/dockerfile/#env), however one environment variable is in the generated docker image
```Dockerfile
ARG DOCKER_PROXY
ENV JAVA_HOME /usr/lib/jvm/temurin-21-jdk-amd64 \
   ORACLE_HOME=/opt/oracle/instantclient_19_27 \
    HTTP_PROXY=$DOCKER_PROXY \
    HTTPS_PROXY=$DOCKER_PROXY
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a Dockerfile with above code
2. Build the image using buildah
3. docker inspect the image


### Describe the results you received

 "JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/temurin-21-jdk-amd64     ORACLE_HOME=/opt/oracle/instantclient_19_27     HTTP_PROXY=     HTTPS_PROXY=     http_proxy=     https_proxy=     NO_PROXY=     no_proxy=",

### Describe the results you expected

JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/temurin-21-jdk-amd64
ORACLE_HOME=/opt/oracle/instantclient_19_27
HTTP_PROXY=
HTTPS_PROXY=


### buildah version output

```yaml
buildah version 1.39.3 (image-spec 1.1.0, runtime-spec 1.2.0)
```

### buildah info output


```toml
default one
```

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: not a bug as I didn't put = between key and value for JAVA_HOME.
